findings.issue: Uses .expect() for QueryPerformanceCounter which creates a potential panic site, though documented as never failing on Windows.
findings.impact: If QueryPerformanceCounter somehow fails, the capture thread would panic rather than gracefully handle the error.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider using .context() with error propagation instead of .expect(), even though failure is extremely unlikely. Alternatively, document that this panic is acceptable since it's contained within the capture thread and the system would need to be severely broken for this to occur.

findings.issue: Initialization wait uses a 2-second timeout with 50ms polling intervals (40 attempts).
findings.impact: Caller could be blocked for up to 2 seconds if WASAPI microphone initialization fails or is slow.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider using a blocking channel or condvar for initialization signaling rather than polling. This would provide faster feedback on success/failure and reduce the maximum wait time.

findings.issue: Doc comment examples use .unwrap() which is not actual executable code but sets a poor example.
findings.impact: Minor - could误导 readers into using .unwrap() in production code.
findings.suggested_fix: Change doc examples to use proper error handling or mark as 'no_run' with better error patterns.

findings.issue: Doc comment examples use .unwrap() which is not actual executable code.
findings.suggested_fix: Update doc examples to demonstrate proper error handling patterns.

findings.issue: noise_thread wrapped in Arc<Mutex<Option<JoinHandle>>> for coordination, but Mutex could be avoided.
findings.impact: Minor overhead for noise thread handle storage. The Mutex is only accessed during start/stop, not the hot path.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider using a dedicated channel or atomic flag for noise thread coordination to avoid Mutex, though current design is acceptable given infrequent access.

findings.issue: insert_sorted uses VecDeque::insert which is O(n) for insertion position.
findings.impact: For queues with up to 32 packets, this is acceptable but could be optimized.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider using a BTreeMap keyed by PTS for O(log n) insertion if queue sizes grow. Current VecDeque with 32 max is acceptable.

findings.issue: DXGI access lost error is properly handled by bailing out and allowing capture_loop to reinitialize.
findings.impact: Good error recovery pattern - the capture loop has exponential backoff reinit logic.
findings.suggested_fix: None - this is a correct pattern.

findings.issue: Proper cleanup of shared fence handles with CloseHandle in Drop implementation.
findings.impact: Prevents handle leaks - the NT kernel handles are separate from COM objects.
findings.suggested_fix: None - this is correct resource cleanup.

findings.issue: NV12/BGRA texture pools use a return channel pattern for recycling GPU textures.
findings.impact: Good design for GPU resource management - prevents unnecessary texture allocation and enables zero-copy encoding.
findings.suggested_fix: None - this is a sophisticated pattern.

findings.issue: nv12_sync_fence and bgra_sync_fence provide GPU-side cross-device ordering without CPU stalls.
findings.impact: Sophisticated approach that avoids CPU spin loops - improves capture rate stability.
findings.suggested_fix: None - this is excellent for AMD GPU performance where VideoProcessorBlt may take >16ms.

findings.issue: Hard cap of 640ms (64 frames) backlog prevents unbounded in_buf growth.
findings.impact: Prevents memory growth if consumer is slow - good defensive design.

findings.issue: Mixer queues capped at 32 packets (~640ms at 20ms per packet) to prevent unbounded memory.
findings.impact: Good memory limit - prevents growth if one stream stops while other continues.

positive_patterns.description: All thread communication uses Arc<AtomicBool> for running flags and bounded crossbeam channels
All major structs have Drop implementations calling stop() for thread and resource cleanup
Unsafe Windows API calls use .ok() for HRESULT conversion and .context() for error messages
BackpressureState uses atomic operations for thread-safe signaling without locks
COM initialization/uninitialization properly guarded with ComApartment RAII pattern
Audio level monitor uses AtomicU32 for thread-safe level tracking without locks
Memory safety checks in capture_frame prevent buffer overflow with overflow-checked calculations

areas_of_concern.status: Good - exponential backoff reinit with max_errors limit
Good - proper bail() with error propagation
Good - atomic flags and bounded channels
